rulings.Case Title: MALCOLM MEYERS VS. CATHOLIC HEALTHCARE WEST et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, June 27, 2012, Line 3. DEFENDANT ROEBBELEN CONTRACTING, INC.'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Sustained with twenty days leave to amend as to the first cause of action and overruled as to the second cause of action. Plaintiff has leave to amend to allege that defendant negligently constructed the ramp and/or controlled the premises at the time of construction. Defendant's remaining arguments lack merit. Defendant's arguments regarding the statute of limitations raise factual issues that cannot be resolved on demurrer. Collateral estoppel does not apply to the discrimination cause of action because the summary adjudication ruling is not a final judgment on the merits. Law of the case does not apply because there was never an appellate opinion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: KAUSHIK DATTANI et al VS. GEEN HONE LEE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, June 27, 2012, Line 6. DEFENDANT GEEN LEE'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Granted. The undisputed evidence demonstrates that plaintiffs last made a payment to defendant in 2009. Plaintiffs' cause of action under Business and Professions Code 7031(b) arose at that time. Plaintiff filed this untimely action in 2011. The one year statute of limitation of Code of Civil Procedure 340 applies because the claim for a refund is a penalty. The delayed discovery rule does not apply because it was not difficult for plaintiff to detect the breach (no contractor's license) and the relationship between the parties does not warrant applying the delayed discovery rule. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: CHINESE HOSPITAL ASSOCIATION, A CALIFORNIA VS. CHINESE MERCHANTS REALTY ASSOCIATION AKA SEE YIP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Pltf And Pltf'S Counsel
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, June 27, 2012, at 9:00 am, line 1, DEFENDANT CHINESE MERCHANTS REALTY ASSOCIATION, AKA SEE Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Pltf And Pltf'S Counsel, Pro Tem Judge Katherine Huibonhoa, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: No tentative ruling; parties to appear. The Judge Pro Tem discloses that she co-chairs the Women's Committee of the Asian American Bar Association of the Greater Bay Area (AABA). Emi Gusukuma of Haas & Najarian, LLP, counsel for plaintiff and opposing party Chinese Hospital Association, is the current President of AABA and nominated the Judge Pro Tem for the Co-Chair position. The Judge Pro Tem does not believe that this association with AABA and Ms. Gusukuma will affect her impartiality. Nevertheless, counsel are invited to raise any concerns with this association by contesting the tentative ruling in accordance with the Court's rules. In the event counsel objects to the Judge Pro Tem hearing of this matter, the matter will be continued to Thursday (7/05/12) at 9:00 am or, if counsel are unavailable on that date, any date thereafter that counsel are available. Any party who contests this matter being heard by Pro Tem Huibonhoa based on this disclosure must send an email to katherinehuibonhoa@paulhastings.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. =302

rulings.Case Title: LOUIE'S CALIFORNIA CHINESE CUISINE VS. SAN FRANCISCO COMMUNITY COLLEGE DISTRICT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, June 27, 2012, Line 10. DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO COMMUNITY COLLEGE DISTRICT MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Granted in part and denied in part. Granted with leave to amend as to the prayer for destruction of, loss of value, loss of use, or repair of real property and the prayer for general damages. Plaintiff is given leave to amend to plead a property interest in the real property at issue and general damages in connection with the nuisance cause of action. Granted without leave to amend as to the prayers for bonus value of leasehold, precondemnation damages, and loss of goodwill but without prejudice to Plaintiff filing an amended complaint to allege such prayers if there later becomes a factual basis to do so. Denied on all other grounds. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: FABIO OLIVEIRA VS. SOUTH BEACH CAFE, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, June 27, 2012, Line 13. DEFENDANTS SOUTH BEACH CAFE, INC., MICHELE D'AMICO AND FERDINANDO BALLARE'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Sustained without leave to amend as to the seventh cause of action and overruled as to all other causes of action and on all other grounds. There is no private right of action for violation of Penal Code section 484. Moreover, the seventh cause of action is duplicative of the eighth cause of action. The individual Defendants are not improperly joined and the eighth and ninth causes of action are adequately pled and not duplicative. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: FABIO OLIVEIRA VS. SOUTH BEACH CAFE, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, June 27, 2012, Line 13. DEFENDANT SOUTH BEACH CAFE, INC., MICHELE D'AMICO AND FERDINANDO BALLARE'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Granted with fifteen days leave to amend as to the prayer for attorney's fees, granted without leave to amend as to the class allegations and the prayer for punitive damages in connection with the first through sixth causes of action, denied as to the prayer for punitive damages in connection with the eighth cause of action for conversion, and off calendar as moot as to the seventh, eighth and ninth causes of action in light of the ruling on the demurrer. Plaintiff concedes that the class allegations are inappropriate. Punitive damages are not available for the first through sixth causes of action. (Brewer v. Premier Golf Properties, LP (2008) 168 Cal.App.4th 1243, 1256.) Plaintiff is given leave to allege, if he can do so in good faith, facts showing that attorney's fees under Code of Civil Procedure 1021.5 are warranted. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)
